meta.setting: Muddy road descending toward French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in road travel and town approach

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Clear Creek road movement and mining-community setting.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Northern California mining road and town texture.

image_prompt.subject: the muddy road into French Gulch with wagon ruts full of sky, pine walls, a small rider silhouette, and smoke from town ahead
image_prompt.scene: cold morning after rain; switchback grade, old wheel tracks, broken fence, ravens over a far ridge, and town smoke showing before the buildings do
image_prompt.composition: square landscape with the road entering from the bottom edge and pulling the eye toward smoke; ruts and puddles act as foreground clues
image_prompt.camera: low road-height perspective, playable route framing, early-HD open-world travel feel
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the road feel used, watched, and ready to make even a short ride expensive.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine route vista
render_traits: slightly simplified rider, trees, and road silhouettes
modest texture resolution on mud, pine, ruts, fence, and shale
hard ambient occlusion in wheel grooves and under fence rails
believable 1899 road conditions and riding gear
travel-pressure composition rather than postcard landscape
style_modifiers.palette: mud umber, rain gray, pine green, smoke blue, wet shale, saddle brown, and cold dawn silver.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable signs, labels, maps, or text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The road, ruts, rider, fence, pine walls, and town smoke are readable.
Every visible prop belongs plausibly to 1899 road travel.
The image makes travel pressure visible before town arrival.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
